prompts.name: GNHF Failure Cluster Repair
prompts.sprintPathRole: Repair one shared root-cause cluster from a noisy test suite
prompts.useThisWhen: AxTask or another issue-heavy repo has many failures that likely share a root cause.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: Failures are unrelated or no exact reproducible command exists.
prompts.inspectFirst: One exact failing command, failure output, related tests, implementation seam, fixtures, and recent changes.
prompts.expectedOutput: One repaired failure cluster with regression proof, or a committed diagnosis report when repair is unsafe.
prompts.nextStep: Use P39 for remaining queued items or P42 to refresh the queue.
prompts.acceptanceGate: At most five related failures are clustered; required failures are not hidden, skipped, or timeout-inflated.
prompts.proofCeiling: Offline failure and regression proof; no broad backlog completion claim.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent "xyz_gnhf_agent_spec" `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 7 `
--max-tokens 700000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "One bounded root-cause failure cluster is repaired with regression proof or a diagnosis report is committed with the exact blocker, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Failure cluster repair
Lane: one shared root cause from a noisy suite

Prerequisite:
- P37 passed for this agent.

Failing command:
- xyz_failure_command

Required checks:
- xyz_required_checks

Objective:
Run the exact failing command once, group failures by evidence-backed shared root cause, select one cluster of no more than five related failures, and repair only that cluster.

Rules:
- Capture the initial failure count and representative errors.
- Distinguish product, fixture, schema, test, environment, and workflow defects.
- Do not chase unrelated failures after choosing the cluster.
- Keep required failures as failures until the root cause is fixed.
- Add regression proof at the narrowest stable layer.
- Rerun the cluster command after each material repair.
- Run xyz_canonical_proof only when the repaired surface requires it.
- Do not convert failures to skips, delete assertions, suppress logs, or raise timeouts to conceal hangs.
- Stop after two bounded repair attempts when the evidence does not improve.

Fallback deliverable:
- If a safe repair is not possible, create and commit xyz_night_report_path with the cluster, evidence, attempted repairs, and exact next command.

Forbidden:
- broad backlog work
- push, merge, release, credentials, live targets, destructive Git

Report:
- initial cluster matrix
- selected root cause
- files and commits
- regression proof
- remaining unrelated failures
- proof ceiling
- git status"

prompts.name: GNHF Stateful App / Game Safe Repair
prompts.moment: GNHF Stateful App / Game Safe Repair
prompts.sprintPathRole: Repair code in a stateful app or game without mutating personal state
prompts.useThisWhen: BlacksmithGuild or another app/game needs overnight fixes but saves, accounts, or live state are sensitive.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: The requested proof requires unavoidable mutation of personal or non-disposable state.
prompts.inspectFirst: Repo safety doctrine, deterministic fixtures, tests, issue evidence, runtime boundaries, and save/account paths.
prompts.expectedOutput: Up to three code-level repairs with deterministic tests and a committed runtime-proof handoff when live proof is still needed.
prompts.nextStep: Use P08 later for disposable live proof; use P42 or P44 after the code lane.
prompts.acceptanceGate: No personal save, account, free-resource, production, or irreversible runtime mutation occurs.
prompts.authority: Code, tests, deterministic fixtures, and safety report
prompts.proofCeiling: Code and deterministic test proof only unless disposable runtime proof is actually observed.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent "xyz_gnhf_agent_spec" `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 7 `
--max-tokens 650000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "Up to three safe code-level issues are committed or blocked, deterministic tests pass, personal and live state remain untouched, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Stateful app or game safe repair
Lane: code, tests, fixtures, and disposable-state boundaries

Prerequisites:
- P37 passed for this exact agent.
- Queue: xyz_night_queue_path

Stateful boundary:
- xyz_stateful_runtime_boundary

Objective:
Repair up to three ready code-level issues without mutating personal saves, accounts, inventories, currencies, free resources, production services, or other non-disposable state.

Rules:
- Read repo runtime and save/account safety doctrine before mutation.
- Prefer unit tests, integration tests, deterministic fixtures, simulators, and disposable temporary state.
- Do not launch the real app or game merely to obtain a stronger proof label.
- Do not use personal accounts, saves, cloud state, multiplayer services, purchases, or free-resource grants.
- Stop and write a runtime-proof handoff when live behavior cannot be proven safely offline.
- Work on one queue item at a time and commit after each completed item.
- Update xyz_night_queue_path and xyz_night_report_path in each commit.
- Bound all waits and test processes.
- Stop after two bounded repair attempts on one item.

Allowed runtime proof:
- only when the repo provides an explicitly disposable fixture, test profile, save, account, VM, container, or sandbox
- record the exact disposable-state evidence

Forbidden:
- personal or production data mutation
- remote PR cleanup, push, merge, release, credentials, destructive Git
- claiming behavior-observed proof from tests or process startup

Report:
- issues attempted and commits
- safety boundary observed
- tests and results
- runtime proof achieved or deferred
- exact P08 handoff when live proof remains
- git status"

prompts.name: GNHF Night Queue Refill
prompts.sprintPathRole: Refill an exhausted or blocked night queue without starting repairs
prompts.useThisWhen: The current committed queue is exhausted, blocked, or no longer matches repo truth.
prompts.doNotUseWhen: Ready items remain in the current queue.
prompts.inspectFirst: Completed dispositions, blocked items, current issue sources, canonical tests, and recent commits.
prompts.expectedOutput: A refreshed committed queue of no more than five new ready items plus preserved history.
prompts.nextStep: Return to P39, P40, or P41.
prompts.acceptanceGate: Completed work is preserved, blocked items remain explained, and no product code changes occur.
prompts.proofCeiling: Queue freshness proof only; no repair claim.
prompts.text:
gnhf `
--agent "xyz_gnhf_agent_spec" `
--worktree `
--max-iterations 2 `
--max-tokens 220000 `
--prevent-sleep on `
--stop-when "The queue preserves all prior dispositions, contains no more than five current ready items, is committed with the refreshed report, and the worktree is clean." `
"Repo: xyz_repo_or_path

Sprint: Night queue refill
Lane: planning only after queue exhaustion or drift

Inputs:
- Queue: xyz_night_queue_path
- Report: xyz_night_report_path
- Issue sources: xyz_issue_sources

Objective:
Preserve completed and blocked history, remove no evidence, refresh stale facts, and add no more than five new ready repair items.

Rules:
- Do not change product code, tests, build files, or runtime behavior.
- Do not reopen completed work unless current evidence proves regression.
- Keep blocked items with exact blocker and next condition.
- Every new item requires evidence, owned scope, dependencies, risk, and an exact validation command.
- Prefer current failing tests and reproducible issues over broad TODO discovery.
- No remote mutation, push, merge, release, branch deletion, credentials, or live targets.

Commit:
- Commit the refreshed queue and report.
- Suggested message: docs(ops): refill bounded night repair queue

Report:
- prior dispositions preserved
- stale items changed and why
- new ready order
- blocked items
- exact next prompt: P39, P40, or P41
- commit SHA
- git status"
